Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.
Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`

## Rules (MANDATORY)
- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.
- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.
- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.
- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.
- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.
- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
- **Path**: `/Users/jason/.local/bin/devdash`
- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking

## Quick Reference
- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`
- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`
- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`
- **Report**: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary="..."`
- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.

## Output Formats
- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.
- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`
- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.

## On-Demand Reference
Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work
- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values

content.text:
Done. Two small changes:

- `client/tailwind.config.js` — added a `logo-pulse` keyframe + animation. Uses the brand-primary blue (`#58a6ff`) as a soft outer box-shadow glow that breathes over 3.5s. Slower and more subtle than the existing `led-pulse`.
- `client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx:67` — added `animate-logo-pulse` to the logo square.

The glow pulses from a faint 6/12px halo to a brighter 14/28px halo and back. Since the logo's fill is already `bg-brand-primary`, the effect reads as the logo itself gently pulsing blue without changing its resting color.

Devdash issue `7a8d2539` is in_progress — let me know when you want to commit/push and I'll close it out. Worth spinning up the dev server to eyeball the speed/intensity; easy to tune the duration (currently `3.5s`) or the shadow radii in `tailwind.config.js` if it's too subtle or too aggressive.

# DevDash CLI Reference

## Commands
  ready [--since=X]                    Pending + unblocked issues sorted by priority. Excludes thoughts. Use this when you need to choose what to work on next.
  list [--status=X] [--since=X]       All issues, optionally filtered
  blocked                              Pending issues with unsatisfied dependencies
  show <id>                            Full issue detail: description, dependencies, pre-instructions, parent. Start here when the user already named the issue.
  find <uuid>                          Cross-project bead lookup (full UUID required)
  stats                                Project health: open/closed/blocked counts
  stale [--since=X]                    In-progress issues with no recent activity

  create --title="..." [flags]         Create a new issue
  update <id> [flags]                  Update an issue
  close <id> [<id>...] [flags]        Close one or more issues
  delete <id> [--force] [--cascade]   Delete issues
  report <id> --status=X [flags]      Report agent progress

  dep add <issue> <depends-on>         Add a dependency
  dep remove <issue> <depends-on>      Remove a dependency
  comment <id> --body="..."            Add a comment
  comments <id>                        List comments
  activity [<id>]                      View activity log

  login [--no-browser]                 Authenticate
  init                                 Initialize project in current repo
  doctor                               Check configuration
  team                                 List team members
  prime                                Output agent workflow context
  version                              Print version

## ID Formats
  Full UUID:    27bf66bd-945f-4714-93fd-0c3322b720f4
  Short prefix: 27bf (any unique prefix)
  Local ID:     dev-dash-42 (project-scoped)

## --since Format
  Nh          N hours (e.g., 24h)
  Nd          N days (e.g., 7d)
  Nw          N weeks (e.g., 2w)
  YYYY-MM-DD  Exact date

## Priority
  0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium (default), 3=low, 4=backlog

## Examples

  # Mark an issue as in-progress
  devdash update abc123 --status=in_progress

  # Close with summary and commit
  devdash close abc123 --summary="Added projectId to request bodies" --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

  # Batch close multiple issues
  devdash close abc123 def456 --summary="Shipped both fixes"

  # Create a child issue under a parent
  devdash create --title="Fix API validation" --parent=abc123

  # Report progress (fire-and-forget, won't fail your workflow)
  devdash report abc123 --status=code_complete --summary="Tests passing"

content.text:
Shipped as `00f5453`. Key changes:

**Root-cause of the clunk**: the old markup flipped layout classes (`justify-center` ↔ `gap-3 px-3`) and conditionally rendered labels, so icons jumped and text popped instead of sliding.

**Fix**: everything now hangs off a **stable `w-16` icon rail**. Logo, every nav icon, the LED stack, and the footer git-branch icon all live inside an identically sized `w-16` flex column. That column's x position never moves — only the space to its right grows/shrinks.

**Labels**: always in the DOM now, animated via `opacity` + `-translate-x-1`:
- On **expand** → `delay-100` so the width transition lands before text fades in
- On **collapse** → no delay so labels clear out before the rail narrows

**Width**: dropped from `w-64` (256px) to `w-52` (208px) per the "too wide" feedback.

**Sync**: both the sidebar `aside` and the AppShell main-content `pl` offset now share `duration-200 ease-out`, so they animate in lockstep instead of drifting apart.

One caveat worth mentioning: the indented sub-items (Tasks, Survey) still shift slightly because `pl-4` on the link pushes the icon right when expanded. Felt subtle enough to ship as-is but if it still reads as janky after you've seen it live, it's a one-line fix to move the indent onto the label only (or use a transform). Let me know.

content.text:
Pushed `4adfcc8`. The pulse now swings between:

- **Rest**: zero halo + logo itself dimmed to `brightness(0.85)`
- **Peak**: three-layer blue halo at `18px/42px/72px` + logo boosted to `brightness(1.25)`

Same `3.5s ease-in-out` timing — only the amplitude changed. The brightness swing on the logo square itself reinforces the shadow swing, so the whole thing reads as a clear heartbeat instead of a faint shimmer. Let me know once Railway rolls it out.
